titleStmt.title: Letter from H. Hallings Latham to Sir Robert Ponsonby Staples, 2 May 1916
Letters 1916-1923
titleStmt.author: H Hallings Latham

editionStmt.p: This work was originally published by Maynooth University in Ireland in 2017. In 2026 this data, stored in a relational database was extracted and converted into this TEI/XML document.

availability.p: This is an open access work licensed under Creative Commons Attribution NonCommercial 4.0 International license (CC BY-NC 4.0).

msIdentifier.repository: Public Record Office of Northern Ireland

projectDesc.p: The data in these XML files was generated based on a data dump from the Magellan database (https://github.com/Maynooth-Center-for-Digital-Humanities/Magellan). Each database record has been converted to a JSON file (https://github.com/letters1916static/letters-data/tree/main/json). The element section of the JSON file contains the TEI-encoded contents of the letters. The TEI XML has been cleaned and made well-formed using a Python script (https://github.com/letters1916static/letters-data/tree/main/src).

body.ab:
H.M.S. 'Glendevon'
c/o G.P.O. May 2nd 1916. Dear Mr. Staples I expect you will have heard by
this time of your son's safe arrival; if
not I believe, as far as I can make
out, the Director of Military Transport
at the War Office is the correct person
to write to. But any how you may
rest assured that he is quite safe,
unless you have heard to the contrary
from the War Office. They would inform
you at once if any thing had happened
to your son anyway & they are
really marvellously quick at letting
relatives know of any bad news,
so not having received this, it is
always a case of the old saying
these days:— "No news is good news" With kind regards, hoping you
& Mrs Staples are in the best of health
& will have good news from your boy Yours sincerely
H. Hallings. Latham.
R. Ponsonby Staples Esqre
Lissan
Cookstown
Co. Tyrone. Comander Latham
